tata.FY2023-24: AMBIGUOUS: Tata FY2022-23 is consolidated while FY2023-24 is standalone

sections.about: This metric measures reported Scope 3 value-chain emissions per rupee of turnover, linking supplier, logistics, investment and downstream emissions to revenue scale. It falls under BRSR Principle 6, Leadership Indicator, because Scope 3 reporting is part of the voluntary leadership disclosure set. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: SAIL leads in FY2024 and FY2025, reaching 4.90e-06 tCO2e/₹ in FY2025 despite a slight deterioration from 4.83e-06 in FY2024. JSW ranks 2nd at 6.91e-06, while Jindal improves to 8.0051e-06 but ranks 3rd because its smaller turnover denominator and stainless value-chain inputs can make Scope 3 intensity appear higher than its absolute emissions. Tata is last at 2.00e-05 in FY2025, driven by the highest absolute Scope 3 total and a significant increase from FY2024. Tata’s FY2025 boundary/methodology notes state that GHG emissions follow the GHG Protocol Corporate and Scope 3 standards and include equity-consolidated JV emissions under Scope 3 category 15.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2025, Tata ranks 4th, with Scope 3 turnover intensity 308% higher than the best performer, SAIL. Tata fell from 2nd in FY2023 to 4th in FY2024 and stayed 4th in FY2025; the FY2023 position is not fully comparable due to consolidated reporting. The FY2025 deterioration from 1.00e-05 to 2.00e-05 suggests a material expansion or increase in value-chain emissions relative to turnover.
sections.targets: Tata Steel discloses supply-chain sustainability actions focused on tracking and addressing Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions, aligned with its Net Zero target. Jindal Stainless reports a 14% reduction in combined Scope 1+2 and Scope 3 emissions from FY2024 and targets Net Zero by 2050.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Scope 3 per rupee is doubly sensitive: first to Scope 3 methodology/category coverage, and second to turnover volatility. Jindal FY2022-23 is missing and Tata FY2022-23 is consolidated, making the first-year interval less directly comparable.
